join_order.notes: Probe pre_by_adsh_tag_ver bucket hash index; no match = passes anti-join. Use MADV_RANDOM on mmap. Use software prefetching: while processing probe i, prefetch bucket_offsets for probe i+8.

scan.access_pattern: sequential within qualifying blocks, two-phase: filter then probe+aggregate

data_structures.uom_dict: load dictionary to find USD code
data_structures.aggregation: CRITICAL: Do NOT use std::unordered_map. Implement a custom flat open-addressing hash map as a struct FlatHashMap with: (1) a keys array of uint64_t initialized to UINT64_MAX as sentinel, (2) a parallel values array of AggState {int64_t cnt; double total;}. Size = 131072 slots (power of 2, 128K). Probe = linear probing with mask. Lookup: slot = (key * 0x9E3779B97F4A7C15ULL >> 47) & mask; while keys[slot] != sentinel: if keys[slot] == key return slot, else slot = (slot+1)&mask. Insert: find empty sentinel slot, write key.
data_structures.selection_vector: thread-local uint32_t sel[100000] array (stack-allocated or vector) holding indices of qualifying rows within each block

parallelism.aggregation_strategy: Each thread owns one FlatHashMap (131072 slots × 32 bytes = 4MB, fits in LLC). After scan, merge all thread-local maps into one global FlatHashMap by iterating slots and skipping sentinels.
parallelism.merge_optimization: Iterate slots sequentially; sentinel check is a single uint64_t comparison. No pointer chasing, no allocation, pure sequential memory access.

indexes.purpose: restrict scan to USD rows only

optimization_notes: Category B+C: The iter_1 code used std::unordered_map despite the plan specifying flat hash maps, and did not implement two-phase scan. Root cause: (1) std::unordered_map causes node-based allocation and pointer-chasing during both scan-time insertion (~2ms overhead) and merge phase (sort_topk=5ms could be ~2ms with flat map). (2) Single-phase scan loads sub_fk/tag_code/version_code for ALL rows including the ~73% eliminated by ddate+value filters. Fix: (A) Implement an explicit custom FlatHashMap struct (NOT std::unordered_map) with contiguous key/value arrays, power-of-2 sizing, linear probing, sentinel-based empty detection. This eliminates allocation overhead and enables cache-friendly sequential merge. (B) Two-phase scan per block: pass 1 checks ddate+value and writes qualifying indices to a selection vector (sequential reads of ddate and value only); pass 2 loads sub_fk/tag_code/version_code only for qualifying rows, does anti-join probe, and aggregates. This reduces column reads by ~73%. (C) Software prefetching for anti-join probes: in pass 2, prefetch the bucket_offsets entry for the next qualifying row while processing the current probe, hiding memory latency for the 153MB index.
